channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: European court rejects 3 Belarusian delisting appeals
item.description: The Court of Justice of the European Union has dismissed 3 appeals against General Court judgments which rejected annulment applications brought by Belaruskali AAT, its director-general Ivan Golovaty

item.title: Memorandum of understanding between US and Iran includes sanctions relief
item.description: A preliminary memorandum of understanding for an end to the conflict between the US and Iran was signed on 17 June 2026, following the parties’ agreement to draft a deal. Both sides will have 60 days

item.title: US charges 1 person with sanctions evasion
item.description: The US has charged a San Diego resident with sanctions evasion for sending money to Hamas and a Hamas fundraising organisation. Reda Sabassi is charged with conspiring to breach the International Emer

item.title: US reaches settlement agreement with Bosch over unlawful exports to Huawei
item.description: The US Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) has reached a $36,184,680 settlement agreement with Robert Bosch GmbH (Bosch), a technology company headquartered in Germany, over 109 breaches of the Expo

item.title: UK Parliament publishes briefing on visa bans
item.description: The House of Commons Library has published a research briefing on the UK’s use of visa bans to exclude foreign nationals on “public good” grounds. Under the Immigration Act 1971 and associated immi

item.title: Canada introduces legislation to ban imports of goods produced with forced labour
item.description: Canada has introduced a Bill to its Parliament that would strengthen Canada’s existing framework aimed at preventing goods made with forced labour from entering the Canadian market – press release; ba

item.title: US updates list of Chinese military companies
item.description: The US has updated its list of Chinese military companies in the US. The list is made pursuant to section 1260H of the 2021 National Defense Authorization Act – press release. Sec. 1260H requires t

item.title: EU member state transposition of the EU sanctions criminalisation directive
item.description: Directive (EU) 2024/1226 on the definition of criminal offences and penalties for the violation of Union restrictive measures entered into force on 19 May 2024. It requires EU member states to prov

item.title: Swiss Federal Supreme Court upholds crypto broker’s freeze of client assets on suspicion of sanctions control pending SECO determination
item.description: The Swiss Federal Supreme Court has dismissed an appeal brought by a cryptocurrency company against a broker’s decision to freeze its assets on suspicion that they were under the indirect control of a

item.title: Corruption sanctions webinar – 18 June 2026
item.description: The Basel Institute of Governance is hosting a webinar on global corruption sanctions on 18 June 2026 at 12.00pm EU time – register here. The panel will discuss how corruption sanctions have develo
